summary:
The insertion allele of the ACE I/D polymorphism lacks direct evidence linking it to insulin resistance; while ACE variants influence hypertension and endothelial function—conditions overlapping with insulin resistance—the specific association of the insertion allele remains unproven. Thus, its status as a candidate gene for insulin resistance is unsupported by available data.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only clinically relevant facts from the original text without adding external knowledge.)
